# Lass Language Reference (for LLMs)

> This file will be auto-generated in Story 7.5 from axioms fixtures.
> For now, this is a placeholder with basic syntax.

## What is Lass?

Lass = JavaScript-enhanced CSS. Write JS in a preamble, use it in styles, get static CSS output.

## File Structure

```
---
// JavaScript preamble (runs at build time)
const x = 1;
---

/* CSS zone with Lass syntax */
.selector { property: value; }
```

## Syntax Quick Reference

| Syntax | Purpose | Example |
|--------|---------|---------|
| `---` | Preamble delimiters | `--- const x = 1; ---` |
| `{{ expr }}` | Expression interpolation | `width: {{ x * 10 }}px;` |
| `@(prop)` | Style lookup (full) | `color: @(--brand);` |
| `@prop` | Style lookup (shorthand) | `color: @--brand;` |
| `$param` | Variable substitution | `font-size: $size;` |
| `@{ code }` | Style block injection | `@{ dynamicStyles }` |
| `//` | Single-line comment | `// stripped from output` |

## Installation

```bash
npm install lass vite-plugin-lass
```

```js
// vite.config.js
import lass from 'vite-plugin-lass';
export default { plugins: [lass()] };
```

## Example

Input (.lass):
```
---
const brand = '#6366f1';
const space = n => `${n * 0.25}rem`;
---

.btn {
  background: {{ brand }};
  padding: {{ space(4) }};
}
```

Output (.css):
```css
.btn {
  background: #6366f1;
  padding: 1rem;
}
```
